source.title: DHA Telehealth Clinical Guidelines for Virtual Management of Conjunctivitis
source.population: Adults and children presenting with red eye or discharge; serious or atypical eye presentations require face-to-face assessment or referral.

source.evidence_paraphrase: Requires history and visual review for red eye and discharge, distinguishes discharge character, asks about vision, foreign-body sensation, photophobia, trauma and contact lenses, and highlights keratitis risk in contact-lens wearers.

items.rationale: Supports contact-lens and allergy history plus clinician-assessed visual-acuity and ocular findings.

items.final_wording: Supports clinician-led management, contact-lens hygiene or cessation discussion, response review, infection-control education, and referral for persistent symptoms, reduced acuity, photophobia, corneal opacity, painful red eye, severe headache with nausea, hyphema, trauma, or inability to open the eye; dose tables are not mapped.

exact_location.heading: Sections 8–9 — management and referral criteria

source.evidence_paraphrase: Supports clinician-led management, contact-lens hygiene or cessation discussion, response review, infection-control education, and referral for persistent symptoms, reduced acuity, photophobia, corneal opacity, painful red eye, severe headache with nausea, hyphema, trauma, or inability to open the eye; dose tables are not mapped.

items.rationale: Supports clinician-documented allergen avoidance, eye hygiene, supportive care, return, worsening, and follow-up without medicine or dose generation.

added_items.final_wording: Describes unilateral or bilateral redness, watery, mucus or purulent discharge, crusting, itching, burning, grittiness, foreign-body sensation, contact spread, dry-eye symptoms, and mechanical or chemical insult context.

source.title: DHA Telehealth Clinical Guidelines for Virtual Management of Conjunctivitis
source.population: Adults and children presenting with red eye or discharge; serious or atypical eye presentations require face-to-face assessment or referral.
source.setting: Telehealth services in DHA-licensed health facilities.
source.exclusions: Direct for Dubai telehealth conjunctivitis and red-eye documentation; partial for broad eye pain, visual change, contact-lens complications, and trauma.

exact_location.heading: Sections 5.1–5.9 — infectious, allergic, and non-infectious features

source.evidence_paraphrase: Describes unilateral or bilateral redness, watery, mucus or purulent discharge, crusting, itching, burning, grittiness, foreign-body sensation, contact spread, dry-eye symptoms, and mechanical or chemical insult context.

added_items.rationale: Supports allergic-conjunctivitis terminology, itch, watering, redness, seasonal or bilateral pattern, and assessed conjunctival, eyelid, or discharge fields.

added_items.final_wording: Lists reduced visual acuity, ciliary flush, photophobia, severe foreign-body sensation, corneal opacity, fixed pupil, severe headache with nausea, post-traumatic red eye, and immune compromise.

source.title: DHA Telehealth Clinical Guidelines for Virtual Management of Conjunctivitis
source.population: Adults and children presenting with red eye or discharge; serious or atypical eye presentations require face-to-face assessment or referral.
source.setting: Telehealth services in DHA-licensed health facilities.
source.exclusions: Direct for Dubai telehealth conjunctivitis and red-eye documentation; partial for broad eye pain, visual change, contact-lens complications, and trauma.

exact_location.heading: Section 6 — red flags

source.evidence_paraphrase: Lists reduced visual acuity, ciliary flush, photophobia, severe foreign-body sensation, corneal opacity, fixed pupil, severe headache with nausea, post-traumatic red eye, and immune compromise.

added_items.rationale: Supports specific vision, severe-pain, photophobia, trauma, corneal, and pupil warning prompts only when assessed.

added_items.final_wording: Requires history and visual review for red eye and discharge, distinguishes discharge character, asks about vision, foreign-body sensation, photophobia, trauma and contact lenses, and highlights keratitis risk in contact-lens wearers.

source.title: DHA Telehealth Clinical Guidelines for Virtual Management of Conjunctivitis
source.population: Adults and children presenting with red eye or discharge; serious or atypical eye presentations require face-to-face assessment or referral.
source.setting: Telehealth services in DHA-licensed health facilities.
source.exclusions: Direct for Dubai telehealth conjunctivitis and red-eye documentation; partial for broad eye pain, visual change, contact-lens complications, and trauma.

exact_location.heading: Section 7 — diagnosis, history, pitfalls, and contact-lens wearers

source.evidence_paraphrase: Requires history and visual review for red eye and discharge, distinguishes discharge character, asks about vision, foreign-body sensation, photophobia, trauma and contact lenses, and highlights keratitis risk in contact-lens wearers.

added_items.rationale: Supports contact-lens and allergy history plus clinician-assessed visual-acuity and ocular findings.

added_items.final_wording: Supports clinician-led management, contact-lens hygiene or cessation discussion, response review, infection-control education, and referral for persistent symptoms, reduced acuity, photophobia, corneal opacity, painful red eye, severe headache with nausea, hyphema, trauma, or inability to open the eye; dose tables are not mapped.

source.title: DHA Telehealth Clinical Guidelines for Virtual Management of Conjunctivitis
source.population: Adults and children presenting with red eye or discharge; serious or atypical eye presentations require face-to-face assessment or referral.
source.setting: Telehealth services in DHA-licensed health facilities.
source.exclusions: Direct for Dubai telehealth conjunctivitis and red-eye documentation; partial for broad eye pain, visual change, contact-lens complications, and trauma.

exact_location.heading: Sections 8–9 — management and referral criteria

source.evidence_paraphrase: Supports clinician-led management, contact-lens hygiene or cessation discussion, response review, infection-control education, and referral for persistent symptoms, reduced acuity, photophobia, corneal opacity, painful red eye, severe headache with nausea, hyphema, trauma, or inability to open the eye; dose tables are not mapped.

added_items.rationale: Supports clinician-documented allergen avoidance, eye hygiene, supportive care, return, worsening, and follow-up without medicine or dose generation.
